Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-30 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
CLMT ranks #1 with an exceptional 96.8 composite score, driven by explosive long-term momentum (162% return over 1 year) combined with consistent ratio progression across timeframes and pristine price action above all major moving averages. The stock's 4.08% single-day gain sits within its characteristic momentum pattern (14.28% weekly, 62.58% quarterly), and its 0.9766 bad-day resilience score shows it holds up remarkably well when SPY declines, suggesting conviction behind the move. DOW shows strong near-term acceleration (76.97% in 3 months, 83.83% in 6 months) with solid ratio consistency, but a dramatic ratio collapse (0.19) from 3-month to 1-year returns and a concerning 2.4457 bad-day score indicate vulnerability during market stress—this is a momentum play tilted toward cyclical strength rather than defensive resilience. The contrast between CLMT's stable, broad-based outperformance (beating SPY/QQQ across all timeframes) and DOW's recent torque with backward-looking weakness suggests very different risk profiles despite both hitting SMA gates. Both stocks are passing technical filters, but CLMT's structural momentum consistency places it in a materially different tier than DOW's near-term cyclical push.
